name: Multiple Sclerosis - Disease-Modifying Therapy Query
description: Tests ability to find multiple sclerosis patients via disease-modifying therapy (DMT) prescriptions. The PheKB MS algorithm uses MS-specific medications (Avonex, Betaseron, Copaxone, Tysabri) as part of the Type 2 case definition. Modern MS DMTs span injectables (interferons, glatiramer), oral DMTs (dimethyl fumarate, fingolimod), and high-efficacy infusions (natalizumab, alemtuzumab, ocrelizumab). Synthea distributes patients across these classes. Unlike RA biologics, MS DMTs are highly specific to MS — Path C should be near-zero for ocrelizumab/dimethyl fumarate/fingolimod, with small overlap only for alemtuzumab (also used in CLL).
prompt: Find patients prescribed disease-modifying therapy for multiple sclerosis, including infusion biologics like natalizumab, alemtuzumab, or ocrelizumab, oral DMTs like dimethyl fumarate, or fingolimod.

prompts.naive: Find patients on multiple sclerosis medications.
prompts.broad: Find patients prescribed any disease-modifying therapy (DMT) for multiple sclerosis. Include high-efficacy infusion biologics (natalizumab/Tysabri, alemtuzumab/Lemtrada, ocrelizumab/Ocrevus) and oral DMTs (dimethyl fumarate/Tecfidera, fingolimod/Gilenya).
prompts.expert: I need MedicationRequest resources for any MS DMT. Use these RxNorm codes: 354770 (natalizumab ingredient), 117055 (alemtuzumab ingredient), 1876366 (ocrelizumab ingredient), 1373478 (dimethyl fumarate ingredient), 1012896 (Gilenya — fingolimod brand). Combine in a single comma-separated code search. MS DMTs are highly specific to MS, so very few non-MS patients will appear in these results — alemtuzumab is the only one with a meaningful non-MS indication (chronic lymphocytic leukemia).

metadata.algorithm_path_description: Patients with MS DMT orders (Paths A and C). The Path C population is intentionally small (~10%) because MS DMTs are highly specific. This makes MS the inverse of GERD — high specificity on the medication side, so the meds query is almost as accurate as the dx query.
